perturbation.explanation: In Section 3, subsection (e), the requirement that the 'Co-Branded Site is available 7 days per week and 24 hours per day' is changed to 'Co-Branded Site is available based on available resources'. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because, elsewhere in the contract, such as in section 12(b)(v), HCI warrants that 'The Co-Branded Site will function, operate and perform in all material respects in accordance with the requirements described or incorporated in the Agreement;' and section 20, which dictates Year 2000 compliance, indicates requirements that can also imply 24/7 availability. Therefore, by changing the 24/7 availability clause to 'based on available resources' introduces ambiguity and a potential breach of warranty should continuous uptime not be maintained. Also, section 3(a) indicates that HCI should 'use reasonable commercial efforts to provide industry accepted standards of reliability of service' This would be contradictory now.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated the 'Co-Branded Site is available 7 days per week and 24 hours per day'. The changed text modifies this to 'Co-Branded Site is available based on available resources'. This creates a strong contradiction with section 12(b)(v), which warrants that 'the Co-Branded Site will function, operate and perform in all material respects in accordance with the requirements described or incorporated in the Agreement'. The changed clause removes the guarantee of 24/7 availability, creating uncertainty and a potential breach of warranty. This constitutes a direct conflict as it makes the site's operational performance no longer strictly in accordance with previously defined requirements.

perturbation.original_text:
4. ADVERTISING
Subject to paragraph 6, MediaLinx shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to Canadian advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement "Canadian advertisers" shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in Canada) and HCI shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to US advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement, US advertisers shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in the United States). All sponsorship and advertising package deals for the Co-Branded Site will be mutually agreed upon by the parties and shall abide by MediaLinx's and HCI's standard advertising agreement and Canadian and U.S. advertising regulations and standards. Any excess inventory that has not been sold is available to be sold by either party. Neither party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to MediaLinx shall be an Internet portal web site and/or an internet service provider. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to HCI shall be a consumer health based web site. The parties shall define a plan in writing prior to the Launch setting out how the Parties shall work together to jointly sell advertising, serve the advertising and track the advertising inventory on the Co-Branded Site.
perturbation.changed_text:
4. ADVERTISING
Subject to paragraph 6, MediaLinx shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to Canadian advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement "Canadian advertisers" shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in Canada) and HCI shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to US advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement, US advertisers shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in the United States). All sponsorship and advertising package deals for the Co-Branded Site will be mutually agreed upon by the parties and shall abide by MediaLinx's and HCI's standard advertising agreement and Canadian and U.S. advertising regulations and standards. Any excess inventory that has not been sold is available to be sold by either party. Neither party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to MediaLinx shall be an Internet portal web site and/or an internet service provider. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to HCI shall be a consumer health based web site.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4 is modified to remove the sentence: 'The parties shall define a plan in writing prior to the Launch setting out how the Parties shall work together to jointly sell advertising, serve the advertising and track the advertising inventory on the Co-Branded Site.' This omission introduces an in-text contradiction because section 2(b), 'promote the Co-Branded Site as set out in Schedule 1 attached hereto;' and section 3(a), 'use reasonable commercial efforts to provide industry accepted standards of reliability of service and to make available the appropriate production resources to build and maintain the Co-Branded Site;' still implicitly refer to a coordinated advertising strategy. By removing the explicit requirement for a written plan, it makes the enforcement of these implicit responsibilities ambiguous, especially concerning the 'reasonable commercial efforts' for promotion, since there's no documented plan for joint action. Without a clear joint plan for selling and tracking advertising, the responsibilities outlined in Schedule 1 could become difficult to enforce.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the sentence 'The parties shall define a plan in writing prior to the Launch setting out how the Parties shall work together to jointly sell advertising, serve the advertising and track the advertising inventory on the Co-Branded Site' directly contradicts section (b) 'promote the Co-Branded Site as set out in Schedule 1 attached hereto' of the contradicted text. Without a documented plan, the obligation to 'promote' as described in Schedule 1 becomes ambiguous and difficult to enforce. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the explicit requirement for a joint advertising strategy is removed, while the responsibility to promote the site remains, undermining the clarity and enforceability of the promotion obligation. This falls under the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text:
20. YEAR 2000 COMPLIANCE
Both MLX and HCI represent and warrant that their contributions to the Co- Branded Site are designed to be used prior to, during, and after the calendar year 2000 AD and will operate during each such time period without error relating to date data, specifically including any error relating to, or the product of, date data which represents or references different centuries or more than a century. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Co-Branded Site: (i) will not abnormally end or provide invalid or incorrect results as a result of date data, specifically including date data which represents or references different centuries or more than one century; (ii) has been designed to ensure year 2000 compatibility, including, but not limited to, date data century recognition, calculations which accommodate same century and multi- century formulas and date values, and date data interface values that reflect the century; and (iii) includes "Year 2000 Capabilities". For the purposes of this Agreement, "Year 2000 Capabilities" means the Co-Branded Site (i) will manage, calculate, sequence, compare and manipulate data involving dates, including single century formulas and multi-century formulas, including leap years and will not cause an abnormally ending scenario within the application or generate incorrect values or invalid results involving such dates; (ii) provides that all date-related user interface functionalities and data fields include the indication of century; and (iii) provides that all date-related data interface functionalities include the indication of century. While HCI asserts that its code and scripts will execute properly, as described above, and that HCI uses third party software that specifies Y2K compliance, we can not guarantee that all third party software will perform as specified by the manufacturer. Should a third party product fail to meet its Y2K compliance policy, we will work with the vendor to remedy the problem in a timely fashion. HCI will ensure that it complies with any third party recommendations that are necessary for Y2K compliance.
perturbation.changed_text:
20. YEAR 2000 COMPLIANCE
Both MLX and HCI represent and warrant that their contributions to the Co- Branded Site are designed to be used prior to, during, and after the calendar year 2000 AD and will operate during each such time period without error relating to date data, specifically including any error relating to, or the product of, date data which represents or references different centuries or more than a century. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Co-Branded Site: (i) will not abnormally end or provide invalid or incorrect results as a result of date data, specifically including date data which represents or references different centuries or more than one century; (ii) has been designed to ensure year 2000 compatibility, including, but not limited to, date data century recognition, calculations which accommodate same century and multi- century formulas and date values, and date data interface values that reflect the century; and (iii) includes "Year 2000 Capabilities". For the purposes of this Agreement, "Year 2000 Capabilities" means the Co-Branded Site (i) will manage, calculate, sequence, compare and manipulate data involving dates, including single century formulas and multi-century formulas, including leap years and will not cause an abnormally ending scenario within the application or generate incorrect values or invalid results involving such dates; (ii) provides that all date-related user interface functionalities and data fields include the indication of century; and (iii) provides that all date-related data interface functionalities include the indication of century. While HCI asserts that its code and scripts will execute properly, as described above, and that HCI uses third party software that specifies Y2K compliance, we can not guarantee that all third party software will perform as specified by the manufacturer.
perturbation.explanation: Section 20 is modified to remove the sentences: 'Should a third party product fail to meet its Y2K compliance policy, we will work with the vendor to remedy the problem in a timely fashion. HCI will ensure that it complies with any third party recommendations that are necessary for Y2K compliance.'. This omission introduces an in-text contradiction because the beginning of the section still says 'Both MLX and HCI represent and warrant that their contributions to the Co-Branded Site are designed to be used prior to, during, and after the calendar year 2000 AD and will operate during each such time period without error relating to date data'. Because there is no follow up after failure with third party products, it is contradictory to what this section implies, if problems or errors arise due to the failure of a third-party product not meeting the Y2K policy compliance. Without the remediation clause, the initial warranty is weakened and potentially breached if a Y2K issue arises from a third-party component.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
20. YEAR 2000 COMPLIANCE
Both MLX and HCI represent and warrant that their contributions to the Co- Branded Site are designed to be used prior to, during, and after the calendar year 2000 AD and will operate during each such time period without error relating to date data, specifically including any error relating to, or the product of, date data which represents or references different centuries or more than a century. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Co-Branded Site: (i) will not abnormally end or provide invalid or incorrect results as a result of date data, specifically including date data which represents or references different centuries or more than one century; (ii) has been designed to ensure year 2000 compatibility, including, but not limited to, date data century recognition, calculations which accommodate same century and multi- century formulas and date values, and date data interface values that reflect the century; and (iii) includes "Year 2000 Capabilities". For the purposes of this Agreement, "Year 2000 Capabilities" means the Co-Branded Site (i) will manage, calculate, sequence, compare and manipulate data involving dates, including single century formulas and multi-century formulas, including leap years and will not cause an abnormally ending scenario within the application or generate incorrect values or invalid results involving such dates; (ii) provides that all date-related user interface functionalities and data fields include the indication of century; and (iii) provides that all date-related data interface functionalities include the indication of century. While HCI asserts that its code and scripts will execute properly, as described above, and that HCI uses third party software that specifies Y2K compliance, we can not guarantee that all third party software will perform as specified by the manufacturer.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a clause that addresses the potential failure of third-party software to meet Y2K compliance, outlining a course of action (remediation with the vendor and compliance with third-party recommendations). The changed text omits this clause. The contradicted text is the initial warranty where 'Both MLX and HCI represent and warrant that their contributions to the Co- Branded Site... will operate during each such time period without error'. By removing the remediation clause, the modified section creates a contradiction because the warranty is no longer supported if a third party causes the failure of the Y2K compliance.
